div1.entryFree: Tower, vb. to fly high, to soar as a bird of prey: which like a falcon --ing in the skies coucheth the fowl below, Lucr. 506. ha, majesty! how high thy glory --s, John II, 350. and like an eagle o'er his aery --s, V, 2, 149. my lord protector's hawks do t. so well, H6B II, 1, 10. a falcon --ing in her pride of place, Mcb. II, 4, 12. --ing == very high: the bravery of his grief did put me into a --ing passion, Hml. V, 2, 80.
